content.claim: Hungary's early selective 6- and 8-year tracks serve higher-status families and students, increase continuation to tertiary education for selectees, but show evidence that students in remaining general tracks lose relative outcomes
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Panel data study not independently specified; details on sample, timeframe, and control variables unavailable from research notes
Winner/loser framing is relative; does not establish whether general-track outcomes declined in absolute terms
Does not isolate tracking effect from selection bias (high-status families may select tracks non-randomly)
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
